name: Acceptable Use Operational Training Augmentation Update
summary: Staged surveillance update to the acceptable-use claim after publication of direct operational state summative training-augmentation evidence and related lower-stakes augmentation and high-stakes detection evidence.

scope.research_question: How should the broader acceptable-use claim change after direct operational state summative training-augmentation evidence and related formative, classroom, and high-stakes detection evidence were published?

proposed_changes.summary: Update the acceptable-use baseline to include controlled operational training augmentation as an acceptable but tightly governed use category.
proposed_changes.rationale: The New Mexico MSSA technical report adds direct operational state summative evidence for limited synthetic response training augmentation, while related sources clarify lower-stakes augmentation and high-stakes detection boundaries.

proposed_claim_implications.note: Adds a narrow controlled operational training-augmentation category to acceptable uses while preserving the boundary against synthetic-only validation, deployment readiness, subgroup fairness, cut-score, or live decision evidence.
Propagates the previously published consequential-use boundary into the broader acceptable-use taxonomy.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
applicability_boundary: Keep the staged unknown_or_unreviewed_contexts and next action to search more operational technical reports and policy documentation.
construct_mapping: Retain the boundary-condition support role for Wang et al.
method_classification: Keep the staged source-specific labels and limitations rather than generalizing across generation methods.
scorer_validation_relevance: Retain the staged interpretive note and does_not_apply_when language.
source_fidelity: Keep the staged summary and limitations that restrict the direct claim to controlled operational training augmentation.
synthesis_overreach: Retain the staged blockers and final-validation exclusions.
